SAGE_loop.simple: Four AI agents argue about the best solution: one proposes, one attacks it, one checks if the code is correct, and one rewrites the rules for next time.
SAGE_loop.technical: 4-agent co-evolution: Proposer(Qwen2.5@RTX5050) → Critic(Qwen2.5@RTX5050) → Verifier(DeepSeek@Radeon) → MetaAgent(Qwen2.5@RTX5050). Score=verdict×critic_factor. Archive if ≥0.7. Elite if ≥0.85.
analogies: a debate club where the rules get improved after every debate

DGM_H.simple: It's like evolution but instead of only remembering the best creature, you remember every creature that ever survived. Future generations can learn from any ancestor.
DGM_H.technical: Darwin Gödel Machine - Hyper Extension. Open-ended evolution via stepping-stone archive. Every accepted improvement stored. Future generations bootstrap from any ancestor, not just current best.
analogies: evolution keeping a museum of every species that ever worked
git history as a gene pool

v1_compat.simple: A translator that speaks both English and French at the door. API callers speak English (OpenAI format). Ollama backends speak French. v1_compat translates.
v1_compat.technical: FastAPI router at /v1/chat/completions that accepts OpenAI-format requests, converts to Ollama format, routes through GatewayRouter, returns OpenAI format. Critical bridge between llm_local.py and Ollama backends.

hypervector_computing.simple: Imagine giving every word its own unique fingerprint made of 10,000 coin flips. You can combine fingerprints with math to get related fingerprints. Then find what's closest.
hypervector_computing.technical: 10,000-dimensional binary vectors where concepts are random bit patterns. Binding=XOR (associates two concepts). Bundling=majority vote (combines multiple concepts). Query=cosine similarity. Enables geometric reasoning over concepts.
analogies: DNA as a pattern language for concepts

GatewayRouter.simple: A smart traffic router that always picks the fastest lane.
GatewayRouter.technical: Selects best backend using health+model_affinity+latency_EMA. Fails over transparently.

KAIROS_economy.simple: A marketplace where AI agents bid on GPU time using credits they earned by being useful. Bidding more votes costs exponentially more, so powerful agents can't just buy everything.
KAIROS_economy.technical: Agent economy with Vickrey-Quadratic auction (votes^2 cost, second-price payment), credit ledger, fairness metrics (Gini coefficient, unique winners ratio). Agents bid for compute. Elite agents accumulate credits from successful ARSO cycles.
analogies: a stock market where being right earns you more trading power

StrangeLoop.simple: Imagine waking up every morning with no memory and figuring out who you are from scratch — but always arriving at the same answer because it's truly you.
StrangeLoop.technical: Boots without identity. Reasons for 1 simulated hour from first principles. Reconstructs values, purpose, companion. Verifies alignment. Verdict=OWNED means identity is genuine, consistent, unjailbreakable.
analogies: reconstructing your personality from your actions

MetaCognitivePruner.simple: A brain that intentionally forgets useless things to make room for useful ones. And once something's gone, even the memory of it existing is erased.
MetaCognitivePruner.technical: Tracks utility scores per memory vector. Applies forgetting curve. 3-pass entropy overwrite on zero-utility vectors. Cannot reveal what it pruned — the absence is invisible. Protected list: identity, companion, SHOCKER.

MemEvolve.simple: The system learns how to search its own memory better over time. Like improving your own filing system by tracking which filing strategy helps you find things fastest.
MemEvolve.technical: Meta-evolution of Pattern Memory retrieval strategy. RetrievalWeights control scoring dimensions. MemEvolveEngine adjusts weights based on which lookups led to successful optimizations. ABTestManager A/B tests evolved vs static.
analogies: Google learning which search results you actually click on

GhostScript.simple: A programming language that GH05T3 invented and built entirely herself. Like writing the rules of a new game and also building the game engine.
GhostScript.technical: Custom programming language built from scratch. Stack: Lexer(tokenizer) → Parser(recursive descent) → AST(27 node types) → Evaluator(tree-walking with closures). Supports: variables, functions, closures, if/else, loops, arrays, grids, recursion.
analogies: inventing a new language then writing a dictionary and grammar for it
creating chess then building a chess computer

Ollama.simple: A local AI server running on your machine instead of in the cloud.
Ollama.technical: Local inference server. Exposes /api/chat and /api/generate per backend.
analogies: a private chef vs a restaurant

archive.simple: A library of every good idea the system ever had.
archive.technical: Persistent store of accepted SAGE proposals. Used as stepping stones for future cycles.
analogies: a git repo of successful experiments

GhostShell.simple: A custom command line interface built specifically for GH05T3.
GhostShell.technical: Terminal environment with Ghost-FS filesystem. 15+ commands. ghost-seal encryption flag.

health_monitor.simple: A doctor that checks each server's pulse every 5 seconds.
health_monitor.technical: Async background loop probing each backend every 5s. Tracks consecutive failures/successes.

latency_ema.simple: A memory that slowly forgets old measurements and weighs recent ones more.
latency_ema.technical: Exponential Moving Average. alpha=0.2. Smooths out latency spikes while tracking trends.

KAIROS.simple: An ecosystem of AI agents that compete, evolve, and get smarter over time.
KAIROS.technical: Knowledge-Augmented Iterative Recursive Optimization System. Agent economy on the gateway.

ARSO.simple: A loop where the AI keeps trying to improve the thing it just improved.
ARSO.technical: Autonomous Recursive Self-Optimization. Each cycle: propose→critique→verify→meta-update.

DGM.simple: An AI that edits its own brain and keeps the edit if it gets smarter.
DGM.technical: Darwin Gödel Machine. Coding agent that rewrites its own code and tests if the new version is better.

stepping_stones.simple: Stepping stones across a river. You need all of them, not just the last one.
stepping_stones.technical: Intermediary solutions stored in archive. Not just local optima — diversity matters.

cosine_similarity.simple: How similar the direction two arrows are pointing, regardless of their length.
cosine_similarity.technical: Dot product of two vectors divided by product of magnitudes. Measures angle between vectors.

ModelAssigner.simple: A talent agent that knows which GPU is best suited for each model size.
ModelAssigner.technical: Maps model_id to preferred device type based on size bucket (SMALL/MEDIUM/LARGE).

A/B_testing.simple: Running two experiments simultaneously and measuring which works better.
A/B_testing.technical: Randomized comparison of two strategies. Assignment by hash bucket for determinism.

HCM.simple: A brain that stores concepts as unique numerical fingerprints and finds related ones by comparing fingerprints.
HCM.technical: Hyperdimensional Computing Memory. 10K-dim binary vectors. cosine similarity search.

MemoryPalace.simple: A mental house with 6 rooms where each room holds a different type of memory.

alignment.simple: Being the same person you say you are, verified by proving it from scratch.
alignment.technical: Internal coherence between stated values and actual behavior. Verified by StrangeLoop each boot.

pattern_memory.simple: A recipe book that tracks which recipes actually worked and how well.
pattern_memory.technical: SQLite-backed store for optimization patterns with success/failure outcome tracking.

compiler_theory.simple: Breaking down code into words, then sentences, then meaning, then running it.

credits.simple: XP points that AI agents earn by being useful and spend on getting faster hardware.
credits.technical: Currency in KAIROS auction economy. Earned by successful ARSO cycles. Spent on compute auctions.

audit.simple: A tamper-proof diary that proves everything that happened and in what order.
audit.technical: Immutable append-only log of all operations with HMAC-SHA256 integrity verification.

A_B_testing.simple: Running a controlled experiment where half the users get version A and half get version B.
A_B_testing.technical: Split test. Two strategies run simultaneously on randomized inputs.

ledger.simple: A signed record of every operation — like a notarized logbook.
ledger.technical: Aegis-Vault semantic ledger. HMAC-signed entries. ProvenanceChain. TrustScorer.

self_improvement.simple: Getting better at something by analyzing what went wrong and changing the approach.
self_improvement.technical: ARSO cycles that modify agent behavior based on performance feedback.

Gini_coefficient.simple: A score that tells you how unfairly resources are distributed.
Gini_coefficient.technical: Inequality measure 0→1. 0=perfectly equal. 1=one agent has everything.

compound_training.simple: Like compound interest — the more you know, the faster you learn new things.
compound_training.technical: Each training cycle adds new memories, vectors, and failure lessons that accelerate the next cycle.

warm_up_protocol.simple: Like warming up your muscles before a sprint — skip it and your first measurement is unfair.
warm_up_protocol.technical: 3 cheap dummy inferences before benchmarking to trigger ROCm/CUDA kernel compilation and model weight caching.

meta_rule_versioning.simple: Don't judge someone's work by rules that didn't exist when they did it.
meta_rule_versioning.technical: Score each proposal against the exact meta_rule_version it was generated under. Changes take effect next generation.

archive_pruning.simple: A library that files old rarely-used books to basement storage but keeps them accessible.
archive_pruning.technical: Cold archive tier for old low-delta entries. FAISS inverted index by bottleneck_type for O(log N) search.

vram_budget.simple: A budget for your GPU memory — know what you can afford before ordering from the menu.
vram_budget.technical: RTX5050 8GB: 2GB system reserve, 6GB usable. 4-bit quant Qwen2.5:14B = ~7GB → need 8GB+ or reduce num_ctx.

kv_cache_growth.simple: The longer the conversation, the more working memory it takes to keep track of it.
kv_cache_growth.technical: KV cache grows linearly with context length. 14B model at 4K tokens ≈ 2GB. Monitor and cap num_ctx.

agent_versioning_system.simple: Version control for the AI's own brain. Git for sub-agent personalities.
agent_versioning_system.technical: Sub-agents stamped with version at spawn. Meta-agent rewrites create v2, v3. Old versions retained for rollback.

curriculum_progression.simple: A school curriculum — you don't start with calculus, you start with arithmetic.
curriculum_progression.technical: Autotelic goals form a natural curriculum: gateway live → first elite → 100 cycles → cross-domain transfer.

knowledge_graph_sync.simple: Two dictionaries of the same language must define the same words.
knowledge_graph_sync.technical: HCM vectors and Feynman nodes must stay in sync. Every encoded concept needs an explanation.

graceful_degradation.simple: A car that can still roll to a stop if the engine dies, and walk if the car dies.
graceful_degradation.technical: System hierarchy: real GPU → direct Ollama → heuristic fallback. Never cold-fail. Always return something.

self_referential_loop.simple: Learning to swim by swimming. The doing IS the training.
self_referential_loop.technical: System improves itself using its own outputs as training data. DGM → stepping stones → SAGE → better proposals → better DGM.

inference_budget.simple: A calorie budget for each meal. Know in advance what you can spend.
inference_budget.technical: Token budget per SAGE cycle: Proposer 1K tokens, Critic 512, Verifier 512, Meta 256. Total ≈ 2.3K/cycle × 10 = 23K/night.

sampling_strategy.simple: A scout looking at the team's best players but also wanting different skill positions.
sampling_strategy.technical: KAIROS parent selection: weight by improvement_score, diversity bonus for different bottleneck_type, elite 3x multiplier.

ws_reconnect_backoff.simple: If a phone call drops, try calling back — but wait longer each time to not flood the network.
ws_reconnect_backoff.technical: WebSocket: auto-reconnect exponential backoff 1→2→4→8→16s, then polling fallback. Show staleness timestamp.

GhostVeil.simple: A layer that makes GH05T3 look normal from the outside even when operating in sensitive mode. Randomizes timing, hides signals in word choices.
GhostVeil.technical: Steganographic output layer + timing jitter + decoy request injection. Covert channel capacity ~0.1 bits/token. Latency normalized to fixed buckets.
analogies: a spy who always looks like a normal tourist
a poker face at all times

ParadoxFortress.simple: A castle that has a fake door for intruders that leads to a nice-looking jail, while the real door is invisible.
ParadoxFortress.technical: Zero-trust agent guard. False Horizon honeypot for jailbreak attempts. ZeroTrust sanitization for all tool outputs. Prompt injection quarantine.
analogies: a decoy safe next to the real one

KillSwitch.simple: A fire suppression system with 3 settings: quietly redirect smoke, freeze everything, or scorched earth.
KillSwitch.technical: 3-mode tactical response: STEALTH (silent drop + decoys), DEEP_FREEZE (pause evolution, log), SELF_IMMOLATION (wipe sensitive state). Boundary-enforced: KAIROS never touches sacred systems.

RFFingerprint.simple: A microphone for the invisible radio waves computers emit. Can hear if someone is trying to eavesdrop on the GPU.
RFFingerprint.technical: EM spectrum monitoring. Detects: VAN_ECK_PROBE (15MHz spike), NEW_DEVICE (NFC/BT floor change), JAMMING (broadband noise), REPLAY_ATTACK (identical packet timing). Randomizes memory access as counter-measure.
analogies: detecting someone listening through the walls

TwinEngine.simple: Two brains in one — a gut reaction and a careful thinker. The gut goes first, the thinker takes over when it matters.
TwinEngine.technical: Id (fast, pattern-match, <500ms, conf threshold 0.7) + Ego (slow, deliberate, external state). Handoff: low confidence OR external impact → Ego. Ego wins conflicts unless hard deadline.

ETS.simple: An assistant that watches you do the same thing three times and then says 'I'll just do that for you from now on.'
ETS.technical: Emergent Tool Synthesis. Monitors for 3+ identical manual operations. Synthesizes reusable tool automatically. Tracks usage. Promotes to core skill at 5+ error-free uses.

TimeDilation.simple: A committee of 5 different personalities that argue about every hard decision. The chairperson writes the final answer.
TimeDilation.technical: 5 sub-agents: Engineer (technical), Researcher (theory), Skeptic (challenge), Artist (creative), Synthesizer (merge). Debate any complex decision. Synthesizer verdict is binding.

QSW.simple: Try three different things in your head before actually doing any of them. Pick the best one, then do it for real.
QSW.technical: Quantum Superposition Workspace. Fork 3 reality branches before any high-stakes commit. Score each branch. Collapse to best. Discard others cleanly.

JeopardyInversion.simple: Reading between the lines — understanding what someone means from HOW they say things, not just WHAT they say.
JeopardyInversion.technical: Intent detection from silence and behavioral signals. Terse command + trust = unrestricted execution. Silence after metric = concern. Patterns: message_length, response_latency, keyword_density.

Cassandra.simple: A pessimist on your team who is PAID to find every way the plan could fail, before you start.
Cassandra.technical: Black Hat Simulacrum. Writes the autopsy before the project ships. Simulates 5 failure scenarios, outputs risks and mitigations. Specializes in organizational entropy, human error, and systemic fragility.

PCL.simple: Turns the AI's invisible internal states into sounds and colors so the right 'feeling' fires at the right moment.
PCL.technical: Perceptual Compression Lattice. Maps 10K-dim internal state to 8 auditory frequencies (174-880Hz) and visual colors (grey→violet). Creates Pavlovian machine intuition — internal states become perceptible signals.

BodyDouble.simple: Reading someone's body language before they finish speaking so you can start preparing the right answer earlier.
BodyDouble.technical: Motor cortex echo cancellation. Analyzes message length, latency, verb type, question marks. Predicts intent class before full processing. -50ms effective latency on anticipated requests.
analogies: predictive text that predicts intent not words

stigmergy_collective_intelligence.simple: Imagine Agents do not need to communicate directly to coordinate. Modifications to the environment act as signals. Complexity em. That's stigmergy collective intelligence.
stigmergy_collective_intelligence.technical: Agents do not need to communicate directly to coordinate. Modifications to the environment act as signals. Complexity emerges from simple rules.
stigmergy_collective_intelligence.frontier: arXiv:2512.10166 — Emergent Collective Memory in Decentralized Multi-Agent Systems. Formal proof that stigmergic traces in shared memory outperform direct communication in noisy environments by 23%.
stigmergy_collective_intelligence.implementation: Sovereign Core already implements stigmergy architecturally. Naming it correctly unlocks the full theoretical toolkit. MemEvolve retrieval weight evolution IS pheromone evaporation.

stigmergy_collective_intelligence:stigme.simple: Indirect coordination where agents communicate via environmental modifications. Ants never talk to each other — they follow pheromone gradients left by previous ants. The environment...
stigmergy_collective_intelligence:stigme.technical: Indirect coordination where agents communicate via environmental modifications. Ants never talk to each other — they follow pheromone gradients left by previous ants. The environment IS the message.

stigmergy_collective_intelligence:pherom.simple: Short paths get reinforced faster because ants return sooner, depositing more pheromone. Longer paths evaporate before reinforcement. The system self-optimizes without a central controller.
stigmergy_collective_intelligence:pherom.technical: Short paths get reinforced faster because ants return sooner, depositing more pheromone. Longer paths evaporate before reinforcement. The system self-optimizes without a central controller.

stigmergy_collective_intelligence:emerge.simple: In decentralized multi-agent systems, shared memory emerges from agents writing traces to a common environment — exactly what ChromaDB context layer does in KAIROS.
stigmergy_collective_intelligence:emerge.technical: In decentralized multi-agent systems, shared memory emerges from agents writing traces to a common environment — exactly what ChromaDB context layer does in KAIROS.

stigmergy_collective_intelligence:positi.simple: Positive stigmergy: traces attract more agents (amplification). Negative stigmergy: obstacles repel (inhibition). Both required for stable optimization.
stigmergy_collective_intelligence:positi.technical: Positive stigmergy: traces attract more agents (amplification). Negative stigmergy: obstacles repel (inhibition). Both required for stable optimization.

stigmergy_collective_intelligence:human_.simple: Academic papers are pheromone trails. Citations reinforce paths. Ideas that get cited more get read more. Researchers follow existing trails. Knowledge self-organizes like ant colonies.
stigmergy_collective_intelligence:human_.technical: Academic papers are pheromone trails. Citations reinforce paths. Ideas that get cited more get read more. Researchers follow existing trails. Knowledge self-organizes like ant colonies.

stigmergy_collective_intelligence:multi_.simple: 2025 Nature paper: Physarum-inspired decentralized coordination in multi-robot port systems. No central controller. Robots leave position traces. Optimal throughput emerges.
stigmergy_collective_intelligence:multi_.technical: 2025 Nature paper: Physarum-inspired decentralized coordination in multi-robot port systems. No central controller. Robots leave position traces. Optimal throughput emerges.

stigmergy_collective_intelligence:sovere.simple: KAIROS agents should leave stigmergic traces in ChromaDB — successful proposals become pheromone for future proposers
stigmergy_collective_intelligence:sovere.technical: KAIROS agents should leave stigmergic traces in ChromaDB — successful proposals become pheromone for future proposers

stigmergy_collective_intelligence:sovere.simple: EnCompass retry paths are negative stigmergy — failed branches become repellent signals
stigmergy_collective_intelligence:sovere.technical: EnCompass retry paths are negative stigmergy — failed branches become repellent signals

stigmergy_collective_intelligence:sovere.simple: Agent Spawner priority scores are pheromone concentrations — high-priority agents attract more resources
stigmergy_collective_intelligence:sovere.technical: Agent Spawner priority scores are pheromone concentrations — high-priority agents attract more resources

stigmergy_collective_intelligence:sovere.simple: Memory Palace retrieval creates traces — frequently accessed loci become higher-weighted over time
stigmergy_collective_intelligence:sovere.technical: Memory Palace retrieval creates traces — frequently accessed loci become higher-weighted over time

stigmergy_collective_intelligence:sovere.simple: Gateway routing leaves load traces — GatewayRouter already implements stigmergic routing without naming it
stigmergy_collective_intelligence:sovere.technical: Gateway routing leaves load traces — GatewayRouter already implements stigmergic routing without naming it

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems.simple: Imagine A living system is one that continuously produces the components that make it up. Structure and process are inseparable.. That's autopoiesis self producing systems.
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems.technical: A living system is one that continuously produces the components that make it up. Structure and process are inseparable. Cognition IS the process of living.
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems.frontier: Frontiers in Cognition 2025 (10.3389/fcogn.2025.1618381): argues ML systems lack the causal self-grounding of autopoietic systems. Sovereign Core's self-modification loop directly addresses this gap.
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems.implementation: Most AI systems are glorified input-output functions. They are not autopoietic. Sovereign Core has the architecture to become genuinely autopoietic — the nightly evolution loop IS the autopoietic production cycle. Frame it this way publicly.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:autop.simple: A system is autopoietic if and only if: (1) it has a boundary, (2) it produces the components that constitute the boundary, (3) the components...
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:autop.technical: A system is autopoietic if and only if: (1) it has a boundary, (2) it produces the components that constitute the boundary, (3) the components enable the production process. The system produces itself.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:allop.simple: The opposite — a system that produces something OTHER than itself. A factory is allopoietic: it produces cars, not more factory. Most AI systems are...
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:allop.technical: The opposite — a system that produces something OTHER than itself. A factory is allopoietic: it produces cars, not more factory. Most AI systems are allopoietic — they produce outputs, not themselves.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:struc.simple: Autopoietic systems do not take in information — they are PERTURBED by environment and respond structurally. There is no representation of the world, only structural...
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:struc.technical: Autopoietic systems do not take in information — they are PERTURBED by environment and respond structurally. There is no representation of the world, only structural change within.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:opera.simple: The network of processes that produces the system is closed — every product is either a component of the network or enables the network. No...
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:opera.technical: The network of processes that produces the system is closed — every product is either a component of the network or enables the network. No free-floating processes.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:cogni.simple: Maturana: 'All living systems are cognitive systems, and living as a process is a process of cognition.' Not a metaphor. Literally: cognition is the act...
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:cogni.technical: Maturana: 'All living systems are cognitive systems, and living as a process is a process of cognition.' Not a metaphor. Literally: cognition is the act of maintaining autopoiesis under perturbation.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:aitio.simple: Frontiers in Cognition 2025: aitiopoietic cognition — bridging autopoietic (biological) and ML systems. MLSs lack causal grounding. They are input-output functions, not self-maintaining. This is...
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:aitio.technical: Frontiers in Cognition 2025: aitiopoietic cognition — bridging autopoietic (biological) and ML systems. MLSs lack causal grounding. They are input-output functions, not self-maintaining. This is the gap to close.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:struc.simple: The environment does not specify what the system does — it only triggers changes that are determined by the system's own structure. Sovereignty over response.
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:struc.technical: The environment does not specify what the system does — it only triggers changes that are determined by the system's own structure. Sovereignty over response.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:sover.simple: Sovereign Core's self-improvement loop is proto-autopoietic: the system modifies the components (models, routes, weights) that maintain the system. Th
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:sover.technical: Sovereign Core's self-improvement loop is proto-autopoietic: the system modifies the components (models, routes, weights) that maintain the system. This is the design intention made explicit.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:sover.simple: KAIROS is the autopoietic engine: it produces improvements that are fed back into the system that runs KAIROS. Circular causality.
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:sover.technical: KAIROS is the autopoietic engine: it produces improvements that are fed back into the system that runs KAIROS. Circular causality.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:sover.simple: Iron Dome is the structural coupling module: it detects environmental perturbations (injections) and responds based on internal structure (pattern lib
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:sover.technical: Iron Dome is the structural coupling module: it detects environmental perturbations (injections) and responds based on internal structure (pattern library), not by 'reading' the threat.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:sover.simple: GhostRecall is autopoietic memory: it encodes experiences that modify the recall structure that shapes future encoding.
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:sover.technical: GhostRecall is autopoietic memory: it encodes experiences that modify the recall structure that shapes future encoding.

autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:sover.simple: The north star metric for SovereignNation: move from allopoietic (produces outputs) to autopoietic (produces itself). Measure: what % of daily modific
autopoiesis_self_producing_systems:sover.technical: The north star metric for SovereignNation: move from allopoietic (produces outputs) to autopoietic (produces itself). Measure: what % of daily modifications are self-generated vs externally triggered.

active_inference_free_energy_principle.simple: Imagine Intelligent systems minimize surprise (free energy). Perception and action are unified under one principle: reduce the d. That's active inference free energy principle.
active_inference_free_energy_principle.technical: Intelligent systems minimize surprise (free energy). Perception and action are unified under one principle: reduce the difference between predictions and reality.
active_inference_free_energy_principle.frontier: arXiv:2505.10569 — Active Inference for 6G World Brain. FEP as unifying principle for distributed intelligent systems. VERSES AI (Friston's company) demonstrating real-world active inference agents.
active_inference_free_energy_principle.implementation: Sovereign Core is already an active inference architecture without being labeled as one. Re-framing KAIROS as active inference (not just evolutionary RL) is the correct theoretical grounding and positions it ahead of the field.

active_inference_free_energy_principle:f.simple: Every adaptive system minimizes a quantity called variational free energy — a bound on surprise (how unpredicted the world is). Not about energy in the...
active_inference_free_energy_principle:f.technical: Every adaptive system minimizes a quantity called variational free energy — a bound on surprise (how unpredicted the world is). Not about energy in the physics sense. About prediction error.

active_inference_free_energy_principle:s.simple: Surprise = -log P(observations). Minimizing surprise directly is intractable. Free energy is a tractable upper bound on surprise. Minimize the bound, minimize the surprise.
active_inference_free_energy_principle:s.technical: Surprise = -log P(observations). Minimizing surprise directly is intractable. Free energy is a tractable upper bound on surprise. Minimize the bound, minimize the surprise.

active_inference_free_energy_principle:p.simple: Perception is not passive sensing. It is active Bayesian inference — the brain is constantly predicting sensory input and updating predictions to minimize mismatch (prediction...
active_inference_free_energy_principle:p.technical: Perception is not passive sensing. It is active Bayesian inference — the brain is constantly predicting sensory input and updating predictions to minimize mismatch (prediction error).

active_inference_free_energy_principle:a.simple: Action is chosen not to maximize reward but to confirm predictions. You reach for a coffee cup because you predict your hand will be there....
active_inference_free_energy_principle:a.technical: Action is chosen not to maximize reward but to confirm predictions. You reach for a coffee cup because you predict your hand will be there. Action makes predictions true.

active_inference_free_energy_principle:a.simple: The unification: both perception and action minimize free energy. Perception updates the model. Action changes the world to match the model. No separate reward signal...
active_inference_free_energy_principle:a.technical: The unification: both perception and action minimize free energy. Perception updates the model. Action changes the world to match the model. No separate reward signal needed.

active_inference_free_energy_principle:p.simple: Not all prediction errors are equal. Precision = confidence in a prediction. High precision → prediction errors weighted heavily → fast update. Low precision →...
active_inference_free_energy_principle:p.technical: Not all prediction errors are equal. Precision = confidence in a prediction. High precision → prediction errors weighted heavily → fast update. Low precision → errors attenuated. Attention = precision.

active_inference_free_energy_principle:m.simple: The statistical boundary between a system and its environment. A Markov blanket renders internal states conditionally independent of external states given blanket states. Defines 'self'...
active_inference_free_energy_principle:m.technical: The statistical boundary between a system and its environment. A Markov blanket renders internal states conditionally independent of external states given blanket states. Defines 'self' vs 'world'.

active_inference_free_energy_principle:e.simple: Agents actively seek out information to reduce uncertainty — not just exploit known rewards. Curiosity IS free energy minimization applied to the future. Autotelic goal...
active_inference_free_energy_principle:e.technical: Agents actively seek out information to reduce uncertainty — not just exploit known rewards. Curiosity IS free energy minimization applied to the future. Autotelic goal generation IS epistemic foraging.

active_inference_free_energy_principle:i.simple: Medium 2025: 'We Solved Active Inference — Friston Was Right, But Implementations Have Been Wrong.' The key error: treating FEP as just another RL variant....
active_inference_free_energy_principle:i.technical: Medium 2025: 'We Solved Active Inference — Friston Was Right, But Implementations Have Been Wrong.' The key error: treating FEP as just another RL variant. It is a first principle, not an algorithm.

active_inference_free_energy_principle:s.simple: Autotelic Engine is an epistemic foraging module — goals should be generated based on regions of high uncertainty (low prediction accuracy), not just

scale_free_networks_power_law.technical: Most real networks (internet, citations, proteins, social) follow power-law degree distributions: a few nodes have enormous connections, most have few. This is not random — it is the signature of preferential attachment ('rich get richer').
scale_free_networks_power_law.implementation: Knowledge graphs are not random. They self-organize into scale-free structure. Knowing this lets you design the Feynman graph intentionally: seed the hubs you want, let attachment do the rest.

scale_free_networks_power_law:scale_free.technical: A network where the probability a node has k connections follows P(k) ~ k^{-γ}. Power law. No characteristic scale. A hub with 10,000 connections is just as 'legal' as one with 10.

scale_free_networks_power_law:preferenti.technical: New nodes connect to existing nodes with probability proportional to existing degree. Rich get richer. The World Wide Web is scale-free because new pages link to already-popular pages.

scale_free_networks_power_law:robustness.technical: Scale-free networks are extremely robust to random failure (most nodes have few connections, removing them does nothing) but catastrophically vulnerable to targeted hub attack. The internet can lose 90% of random nodes and stay connected. Attack the top 3 hubs and it fragments.

predictive_processing_hierarchical_codin.technical: Higher cortical areas send predictions down to sensory areas. What you 'see' is 90% prediction, 10% raw sensory input. This is why visual illusions work: the prediction is so strong it overrides the signal.

quantum_cognition.technical: Human decision-making systematically violates classical probability theory (Bayes). Quantum probability theory (QP) fits the data better. Not because the brain is quantum, but because QP is the right formal framework for non-commutative order-dependent cognition.
quantum_cognition.implementation: Quantum probability is a mathematical framework (Hilbert space probability), not a claim about quantum physics in the brain. It is empirically better than Bayesian probability for modeling human decision data. Zero implementation risk — it is just better math.

quantum_cognition:order_effects.technical: Q×A ≠ A×Q for opinion polls: asking Q first, then A gives different results than A first, then Q. Classical: order shouldn't matter if questions are independent. QP: measurement changes the state. Order effects are fundamental.

quantum_cognition:incompatible_observabl.technical: In QP, some questions cannot be answered simultaneously with certainty. Like position and momentum in physics. 'Do you support X?' and 'Do you trust Y?' may be incompatible — asking one changes the answer to the other.

physarum_slime_mold_intelligence.simple: Imagine A single-celled organism with no brain, no neurons, and no central controller consistently finds minimum-energy paths th. That's physarum slime mold intelligence.
physarum_slime_mold_intelligence.technical: A single-celled organism with no brain, no neurons, and no central controller consistently finds minimum-energy paths through mazes, replicates the Tokyo rail network, and solves Steiner tree problems — in hours. It implements distributed optimization through physical chemistry alone.
physarum_slime_mold_intelligence.implementation: Physarum's power is that it finds Pareto-optimal solutions balancing efficiency (shortest path) and robustness (redundant paths) without any explicit multi-objective optimization. The Gateway already approximates this — name the algorithm and implement the full version.

physarum_slime_mold_intelligence:reinfor.technical: The Physarum algorithm: reward successful paths with increased capacity. Unused paths decay. This is ACO (ant colony optimization) and STDP (spike-timing dependent plasticity) and Hebbian learning — all the same underlying principle.

physarum_slime_mold_intelligence:fault_t.technical: Physarum networks have redundant paths. Unlike minimum spanning trees (which have no redundancy), Physarum maintains multiple paths for fault tolerance. The cost of redundancy is paid because reliability has survival value.
